answer.content:
<html>
<head>
    <title>Graph (Undirected)</title>
    <style>
        #graph {
            border: 1px solid black;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <canvas id="graph" width="400" height="400"></canvas>
    <script>
        const canvas = document.getElementById('graph');
        const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');

        const numVertices = 10;
        const edges = [];

        function addVertex() {
            const vertex = {
                x: Math.random() * canvas.width,
                y: Math.random() * canvas.height,
            };
            edges.push(vertex);
            drawVertex(vertex);
        }

        function drawVertex(vertex) {
            ctx.fillStyle = 'rgb(0, 0, 255)';
            ctx.beginPath();
            ctx.arc(vertex.x, vertex.y, 10, 0, 2 * Math.PI);
            ctx.fill();
        }

        function addEdge(from, to) {
            edges.push({ from, to });
        }

        function drawGraph() {
            ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
            for (const edge of edges) {
                ctx.beginPath();
                ctx.moveTo(edge.from.x, edge.from.y);
                ctx.lineTo(edge.to.x, edge.to.y);
                ctx.stroke();
            }
        }

        function update() {
            drawGraph();
            addVertex();
            addEdge(Math.floor(Math.random() * numVertices), Math.floor(Math.random() * numVertices));
        }

        setInterval(update, 1000);
    </script>
</body>
</html>

messages.content: Generate a single HTML file with JavaScript demonstrating the concept Graph (Undirected). Only respond in a single HTML file.
